title: jacobaea paludosa subsp. angustifolia
intro: Jacobaea paludosa subsp. angustifolia, also known as marsh marsh marigold, is a flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Jacobaea paludosa subsp. angustifolia include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as asthma and bronchitis. The plant can be prepared as an infusion or tincture for medicinal use, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively studied and its use should be approached with caution.
